text.title: 30-60-90-day-plan Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for 30-60-90-day-plan as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using the same plan template for engineering and sales roles without role-specific adaptation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A Sales 60-day milestone (50% pipeline) is fundamentally unlike an engineering one (feature delivered). Generic templates produce milestones that don't match either role's actual delivery cycle, making the review a formality.

failure-mode.description: Setting stretch goals in the Learn phase (days 1-30) to signal high expectations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stretch goals in the first 30 days reward speed over quality, incentivize the hire to skip context-building, and produce poor output that creates downstream debt. The manager signals expectations through the day-60 and day-90 milestones, not by front-loading delivery.

failure-mode.description: Plans written solely by HR using a generic template, then handed to the hire on Day 1 as a done document.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Plans the hire did not co-author become external checklists with no ownership. Drift starts in week 2; by day 30, nobody references them. Thirty minutes of co-authoring on Day 1 produces a plan the hire treats as a personal commitment.
